name: Houthis (Yemen)
summary: Day 76-77 quiet — UKMTO and Lloyd's List confirm no Houthi attacks on commercial shipping recorded in the Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb / Gulf of Aden during Iran war's 2026 phase. Maritime risk remains 'moderate' (not critical) for Bab el-Mandeb. **Houthi non-response continues through both the May 4 multi-axis Iran kinetic restart AND the MOU-framework cycle.** Houthi leadership has issued threatening rhetoric since February 28 but no operational action. **The non-action despite Iran's May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart confirms the coordination-decay thesis:** Iran's ability to direct proxy operations has weakened. The pattern is now corroborated by Hezbollah's non-retaliation against the May 6 Beirut strike on Radwan commander Ballout — both proxies show coordination-decay relative to historical baseline. Persistent VLCC/Frontline rate elevation is HORMUZ-driven, NOT Bab el-Mandeb-driven. Maritime Executive even reports 'Houthis Announce End of Red Sea attacks' — though this announcement has not been formally verified via state action.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis begin confirmed Red Sea attacks on NEUTRAL commercial shipping (verified by maritime trackers) — Day 47 attacks RESUMED but ONLY on belligerent-linked vessels (US/UK/Israeli). Neutral shipping
Houthis launch confirmed attack on Israel (missile/drone reaching Israeli territory)

DAY 64 RED SEA QUIET — UKMTO UPDATE 034 EXPLICITLY DOCUMENTS HOUTHI RHETORIC-WITHOUT-OPERATIONAL-INDICATORS

historical_summaries.summary: 10 signals. Abdul Malik al-Houthi declared group 'prepared to enter the war' with 'fingers on the trigger' in recorded address | Mohammed al-Bukhaiti (political bureau member, not military commander) told NYT: 'the expansion of the conflict to include other countries, | Three weeks into war, no Houthi attacks

22 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | CONFIRMED ABSENCE: Day 34 of war — ZERO confirmed attacks on merchant shipping in Red Sea or Bab el-Mandeb | Day 35: ZERO confirmed Red Sea shipping attacks
